In exile, they struggle to mount an international women's movement to pressure world leaders to collapse the as yet unrecognized Taliban government, while coming to terms with what it means to have their power usurped and two decades of progress dismantled.

programme.title: Tell Them You Love Me
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of esteemed university professor Anna Stubblefield's controversial affair with Derrick Johnson, a non-verbal man with cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Barbie Uncovered: A Dreamhouse Divided
programme.desc: The story of the two people responsible for creating the Barbie doll - Ruth Handler, the Jewish immigrant who built the largest toy company in the world from nothing and designer Jack Ryan, the man said to have actually created the doll.

programme.title: Villeneuve Pironi: Racing's Untold Tragedy
programme.desc: The relationship between F1 drivers Gilles Villeneuve and Didier Pironi, team-mates whose friendship was torn apart after a controversial ending to the 1982 San Marino Grand Prix.

programme.title: Forced Out
programme.desc: The stories of men and women dishonourably discharged from the Army, Navy and RAF because of their sexuality, with many imprisoned, having medals removed or losing their service pensions.

programme.title: Adam Hills: Foot Fault
programme.desc: The comedian and host of The Last Leg takes viewers behind the scenes of a global campaign to promote para-standing tennis and its potential as a future Grand Slam and Paralympic event. Adam meets and plays with former Grand Slam winners and uncovers the barriers faced by aspiring tennis players with disabilities who want to be included by their sport at the highest level.

programme.title: Discovering: Sigourney Weaver
programme.desc: The life and career of the American actress, who rose to fame in Ridley Scott's Alien and was nominated for an Acacdemy Award when she reprised her role in Aliens in 1986.

programme.title: David Frost Vs
programme.desc: The interviewer's encounter with actress and campaigner Jane Fonda, during her protests about the Vietnam War.

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before.

programme.title: Busby
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best.

programme.title: JFK Revisited: Through the Looking Glass
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's documentary examining the assassination of US president John F Kennedy, using James DiEugenio's 1992 book Destiny Betrayed: JFK, Cuba and the Garrison Case, and recently declassified US government files to shine a light on what happened in Dallas on November 22, 1963. Narrated by Whoopi Goldberg and Donald Sutherland.

programme.title: I Am Jackie O
programme.desc: Profile of Jackie Kennedy Onassis, providing a definitive exploration of her life, from first lady to widow, unofficial royalty and fashion icon.

programme.title: I Am Patrick Swayze
programme.desc: A tribute to the American actor, known for his roles in hits like Dirty Dancing, Ghost and The Outsiders. With contributions by Demi Moore and Rob Lowe among others.

programme.title: My Icon: Shaun Gayle
programme.desc: The former Chicago Bears defensive back and Super Bowl winner discusses the individuals that have inspired him during his life.

programme.title: I'm Chevy Chase and You're Not
programme.desc: Chevy Chase was a cast member on the inaugural series of Saturday Night Live and he built on that success to star in numerous Hollywood comedies and host the Academy Awards. He played hapless father Clark Griswold in the National Lampoon films and ran amok in pictures including Caddyshack, Spies Like Us, Fletch and Three Amigos. Two-time Emmy Award winner Marina Zenovich directs an authorised feat

programme.title: I Am Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A celebration of the country singer-songwriter built around 12 of his classic tracks spanning four decades, and featuring interviews with his family and friends. Featuring contributions from Sheryl Crow, Kris Kristofferson, Willie Nelson, John Mellencamp and Kid Rock.

programme.title: My Icon: Leon Pryce
programme.desc: The former Bradford Bulls and St Helens star discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and sporting career.

programme.title: Blur: To the End
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the relationship of the band's Damon Albarn, Graham Coxon, Alex James and Dave Rowntree over the course of three decades, unfolding against the backdrop of the quartet collaborating on 10 new songs ahead of two sold-out concerts at Wembley Stadium.

programme.title: Fatboy Slim: Right Here, Right Now
programme.desc: Documentary about the Big Beach Boutique II free event held in 2002, when an estimated quarter of a million people turned up to the concert on Brighton beach.

programme.title: Music Box: Counting Crows
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the rock band and singer Adam Duritz navigated sudden fame while staying true to their art after the success of their debut album.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: A look at how canids and felines deploy an arsenal of hunting skills. Felines face endurance, while wolves rely on their privileged sense of smell in a fight for survival.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters

programme.title: Africa's Wild Year

programme.desc: In 1997, black rhinos were declared extinct in Zambia. Thanks to an ambitious reintroduction plan, they are back. This documentary follows the team fighting to keep them safe.

programme.desc: The team races to contain a case of malaria brought in by orphaned infant Cesia, before assessing surrogate mum Sri's plan to help Bumi.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild

programme.desc: As the land dries up, matriarch elephant Anura races to get her herd to a distant watering hole. But her tiny grandson is struggling to keep up.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Attenborough's Journey
programme.desc: Documentary following David Attenborough as he journeys around the world to make his series First Life, which explores the origins of life on Earth. programme.title: David Attenborough's Dragons and Damsels
programme.desc: Arguably more beautiful than butterflies, better fliers than hummingbirds and with intriguing and complex behavioural patterns - dragonflies and damselflies are marvels of the insect kingdom. This documentary uses the latest camera technology to show their manoeuvres, mating strategies, territorial behaviour and flight dynamics, revealing them to be the ultimate insect.
